rag.hyde_answer: A for loop in Python repeats a block of code for each item in a sequence such as a list or string.
rag.ground_truth_concept_card: For loops in Python are used to iterate over sequences and execute indented code for each item.
chromadb_chunks: A Python for loop iterates over each item in a sequence.
The loop variable takes one value at a time from the iterable.
Indentation defines the body of the loop.

tavily_results.title: Python For Loops
tavily_results.content: Python for loops are used for iterating over lists, strings, ranges, and other iterables.

chunks_before_rerank: A Python for loop iterates through items in a sequence.
chunks_after_rerank: A Python for loop iterates through items in a sequence.
The loop variable changes on each iteration.
rag.lesson_text: A for loop lets Python repeat work for every item in a sequence. For example, for fruit in fruits: print(fruit) prints each fruit one by one.

lesson.lesson_text: A for loop lets Python repeat work for every item in a sequence. Example: fruits = ['apple','banana']; for fruit in fruits: print(fruit). Python prints each item one by one.

questions.question: What does a for loop do in Python?
questions.source_quote: A for loop lets Python repeat work for every item in a sequence.

qa_log.question: What does a for loop do in Python?
qa_log.answer: It repeats code for each item in a list or sequence.
